name: Andrej Karpathy
role: former Tesla AI Director; OpenAI founding team
summary: Argues the hard part of agent-built products is now the surrounding DevOps stack - payments, auth, databases, security, domains, and deployment. The milestone to watch is an agent rebuilding and shipping a full product end to end, not just generating code.

name: Swyx
role: builder, podcaster, and developer advocate across DX/agent tooling
summary: Says coding agents create a golden age for people who have documented their workflows, because years of setup notes can now be converted into executable scripts and reusable skills. He published his 2026 Mac setup as a Claude-consumable skill.

name: Josh Woodward
role: VP at Google, Google Labs, Gemini, and Google AI Studio
summary: Highlighted Gemini features aimed at retention and usability: importing chats and memory from other chatbots, plus a major Gemini Live upgrade with faster responses, better EQ, broader language range, and longer context.

name: Kevin Weil
summary: Used a case involving ChatGPT and AlphaFold helping create a personalized mRNA vaccine protocol for a dog to illustrate how AI is compressing advanced biomedical reasoning into workflows individuals can use.

name: Amanda Askell
summary: Argued remote work may have worsened conditions for people who prefer offices, because companies now treat home as the fallback instead of fixing poor workspaces. She also noted that private offices may be a more powerful recruiting lever than many flashy perks.

name: Thariq
summary: Announced peak-hour session limit changes where weekly limits stay the same but usage burns faster during weekday high-demand windows, signaling Anthropic is using scheduling and efficiency improvements to manage growth.

name: Amjad Masad
summary: Framed vibecoding as a new unlock for entrepreneurship and argued platforms should not gatekeep the category, pointing to a coming policy and ecosystem battle over who gets to build with conversational software creation tools.

name: Guillermo Rauch
summary: Argued that useful agents need real computers - tool access, install rights, write/run/debug loops, deployment paths, and persistence. His Vercel Sandbox pitch is that ephemeral tool-calling alone is not enough for serious agent work.

name: Aaron Levie
summary: Said coding agents are becoming the backbone of enterprise knowledge-work automation and that Box wants to plug enterprise content directly into that loop via its Codex plugin. He also stressed that change management, legacy systems, weak APIs, and missing context remain the real barriers to enterprise AI adoption.

name: Zara Zhang
summary: Shared that product ideas emerge less from isolated thinking and more from playful experimentation with models plus frequent conversations with users, founders, and developers.

name: Nikunj Kothari
summary: Showed a concrete agent workflow using Claude Code, Google Calendar MCP, and the Ramp CLI to finish three months of expense categorization in about 12 minutes, highlighting how agents are already useful in operational finance tasks.

name: Aditya Agarwal
role: General Partner at South Park Commons
summary: Amplified Elad Gil's contrarian startup arguments that more markets are open than founders think, product surface area can beat data as a moat, and a lot of startup conventional wisdom is wrong in the current AI cycle.
